data.title: Report on Australia's mining fiscal landscape in 2025 includes governing bodies, laws, mineral licenses, and fees by state/territory.
data.description: The report titled "Australia Mining Fiscal Landscape: Regulations, Governance and Sustainability (2025)" recently added to ResearchAndMarkets.com, provides insights into the regulatory framework governing the mining sector in Australia. It delves into the roles and responsibilities of federal and state governments in resource exploration and development. The report details the governing bodies, laws, mineral licenses, and fees across different states and territories. Mineral resources are predominantly owned by the Australian government, as well as state or territory entities.

data.title: Dow Jones gains over 100 points, US jobless claims decline by 10,000 to 214,000. Market sentiment improves.
data.description: This morning, U.S. stocks showed positive movement as the Dow Jones index surged over 100 points. As of Wednesday's market opening, the Dow was up by 0.28% to 48,576.79, the NASDAQ rose by 0.02% to 23,567.10, and the S&P 500 increased by 0.15% to 6,920.17. Notably, consumer staples shares saw a 0.6% rise, while communication services stocks experienced a 0.2% decline. In recent jobless claims data, the U.S. reported a decrease of 10,000 from the previous week to 214,000 claims for the week ending Dec. 20, surpassing market expectations set at 223,000 claims. For further details, the full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Ukraine construction industry grew by 21.7% in 2024, expected to slow to 4.2% in 2025 due to Russia's invasion impacts.
data.description: In Dublin, a report on the Ukraine construction market has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. The report covers sectors such as commercial, industrial, infrastructure, energy and utilities, institutional, and residential markets, with analysis up to 2029 (H2 2025). After experiencing a 21.7% growth in 2024, the construction industry is projected to slow down to 4.2% in 2025. This deceleration is attributed to the lingering effects of Russia's invasion impacting investment and business operations.

data.title: Hungary construction industry to contract by 2% in 2025 due to slowdown in real estate and industrial construction.
data.description: A report on the Hungary Construction Market projects a slight contraction of 2% in the industry in 2025, attributed to reduced activity in real estate and industrial construction. The analysis covers sectors such as Commercial, Industrial, Infrastructure, Energy and Utilities, Institutional, and Residential markets. The findings anticipate this decline to continue throughout 2029. The report has been incorporated into ResearchAndMarkets.com, offering insights into the market size, trends, and forecasts for the construction industry in Hungary.

data.title: XRP falls below $2: Negative sentiment indicates potential for strong rebound despite facing resistance.
data.description: The sentiment surrounding XRP has reached extremely fearful levels, a situation that has previously led to significant rebounds. However, XRP is encountering strong resistance as it attempts to recover.

data.title: AST Spacemobile's BlueBird 6 launch from India boosts stock, seeks to add to 300% advance. - Investor's Business Daily
data.description: Late on Tuesday, AST Spacemobile launched its BlueBird 6 from India, causing a surge in the space stock. Following the successful India launch, AST Spacemobile aims to build on a 300% increase in value.

data.title: 25% of American workers are 'functionally unemployed', facing struggles with low hours and wages according to Ludwig Institute.
data.description: In November 2025, the true rate of unemployment in the United States reveals that approximately 25% of American workers are considered 'functionally unemployed.' This indicates that a quarter of the workforce is struggling with low hours and wages, showcasing ongoing challenges in the labor market. The data suggests persistent difficulties for a significant portion of the workforce, pointing towards continued economic strain.
